title: Leaving Notes 📝
description: Learn how to use comments to document your code.

slides.title: Talking to Humans 🗣️
slides.content:
Compilers read code to talk to the computer. But what about talking to yourself or other programmers?

**Comments** are ignored by the compiler entirely. They exist solely for humans to read.

slides.title: Single-Line Comments
slides.content: Use two forward slashes `//` to create a single-line comment. Everything after the slashes on that line is ignored.

quizData.question: Which of the following lines will the C compiler ignore?
quizData.explanation: The line starting with `//` is a comment and is completely ignored by the compiler. (Notice that the printf is valid code printing the slashes).

slides.title: Multi-Line Comments
slides.content:
If you need a really long comment, you don't have to put `//` on every line.

Start a block with `/*` and end it with `*/`.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Comment the 'Why', not the 'What'

Don't write comments like `// adds 1 to x`. It is obvious from reading `x = x + 1;`.

Instead, explain *why* you are doing it: `// compensate for zero-indexed array`.
